Name: Feedback — Need feedback
Headline: Need feedback on your book?
Primary text: Your friends say they loved it. They're being kind. Get eight honest readers who'll tell you the truth, and show you the exact page where attention drops.

Name: Where readers give up
Headline: Where do readers give up?
Primary text: Nobody messages you to say they put your book down. They just stop. Now you can see the chapter it happens, and fix it before anyone leaves a review.

Name: It read every chapter
Headline: It read every chapter.
Primary text: You shouldn't have to re-explain your own book every time you sit down to write. Ask about a character from chapter two while you're deep in chapter forty, and you get a real answer.

Name: Your whole book in one place
Headline: Your whole book, in one place.
Primary text: Chapters in one file. Notes in another. The outline lost in a folder somewhere. Slima keeps the whole book together, and remembers where you left off.

Name: Version control
Headline: Version control for your story.
Primary text: Every writer has cut a paragraph they secretly wish they'd kept. Here you never have to. Branch a chapter, try the bold version, and the original is always one click away.

Name: Eight readers
Headline: Eight readers. One honest read.
Primary text: One catches plot holes. One is impossible to impress. One quits the moment a scene drags. Eight readers, telling you what a kind friend never will.

Name: Testimonial
Headline: Never forgets where I left off
Primary text: "I used to lose an hour just finding the right note. Now my whole book lives in one place, and it never forgets where I left off." A. R., novelist.

Name: Before launch
Headline: Find the slow chapter before launch.
Primary text: The slow chapter is a lot cheaper to fix in a draft than in your reviews. Eight honest readers tell you which one it is, and why.

Name: Keep the series straight
Headline: Keep the whole series straight.
Primary text: Holding a whole season in your head is how the finale stops adding up. Slima tracks every character and beat from the pilot on, so the callback still lands.

Name: Finish your book
Headline: Finish the book you've carried for years.
Primary text: You've been carrying this book for years. Give it one place built to get it done. Write it, test it, and publish it without losing the thread.

Name: Every source in one place
Headline: Every source and draft, in one place.
Primary text: Notes in one app. PDFs in another. Drafts all over your desktop. Slima keeps the whole paper together, sources and all, so you stop hunting and start writing.

Name: Argument check
Headline: Write the long paper without losing the thread.
Primary text: By section five it's hard to remember exactly what you claimed in section two. Slima holds the whole argument at once, so the paper actually hangs together.

Name: Ask your own book
Headline: Ask anything about your own book.
Primary text: "Is Elena's motive consistent?" Ask, and the answer comes straight from your own chapters, because it has read every one of them.

Name: Watch the finish line
Headline: Watch the finish line get closer.
Primary text: Open it, write a little, watch the number climb. A steady streak is how a draft you've stalled on quietly turns into a finished one.

Name: Your whole world remembered
Headline: Your whole world, remembered.
Primary text: Forty characters, three kingdoms, a magic system with actual rules. Slima keeps your whole world straight so you can just write inside it.

Name: Catch the one-star
Headline: Catch the one-star before it happens.
Primary text: "I gave up around chapter four." That's a review you can prevent. Slima points to the slow scene while you can still do something about it.

Name: Readiness scorecard
Headline: Know it's ready before you publish.
Primary text: Hit publish knowing it's ready, not just hoping. Get a clear read on pacing, continuity, and whether readers make it to the end.

Name: Coverage before the table read
Headline: Coverage before the table read.
Primary text: Hear the notes in private first. Every scene gets an honest read on stakes, dialogue, and pacing before a single person says it out loud.

Name: A whole series one structure
Headline: A whole series, one structure.
Primary text: Your whole show in one place, shaped the way it actually is. Season, episode, scene, with every character tracked across all of it.

Name: Never lose a citation
Headline: Never lose a citation.
Primary text: It's midnight and you're rebuilding your reference list again. Never again. Slima tracks every source, quote, and page number as you write.

Name: Revise for your advisor
Headline: Revise for your advisor. Lose nothing.
Primary text: Apply the notes. Try the bigger revision. Go back to the version the committee liked. Every draft of the dissertation is saved, so nothing you write is ever gone.

Name: UGC — before you publish
Headline: Before I published I had no idea readers were quitting
Primary text: Talking-head UGC: a writer says she had no idea readers were quitting halfway through chapter four until she ran a reader report. Add burned-in captions before posting.
